name: Communion
description: The final confrontation with the Heartwood — the ancient forest consciousness that has held Ashvale in a blood pact for three centuries. Three paths. Three futures. One choice that cannot be undone.

ACTIVE.journal_entry: The Heartwood has laid bare the truth: three hundred years of broken promises, nine souls given where hundreds were owed. The forest is dying of starvation, and it demands resolution. I must choose.
objectives: Listen to the Heartwood's account of the pact
Choose: Fulfill the Pact (sacrifice), Sever the Pact (druidfire), or Renegotiate

COUNTER_RITE_PATH.journal_entry: The counter-rite — Old Maren's alternative. Every secret this village has buried must be spoken aloud at the site of the original pact. The truth itself is the offering.
objectives: Speak all discovered truths at the blood-root

PACT_DISSOLVED_THROUGH_TRUTH.journal_entry: The names of the sacrificed echoed through the Heartwood. Every truth spoken was a root loosened, a chain broken. The pact did not burn or sever — it simply dissolved, like a lie exposed to daylight. The forest shuddered, and then was still. Not dead. Not angry. Just... free.

PACT_UNRESOLVED.journal_entry: The counter-rite failed. Not enough truths were spoken — or the truths I knew were not enough. The pact holds. The Heartwood waits, patient and hungry as ever.

SACRIFICE_PATH.journal_entry: I have chosen to honor the pact. A willing sacrifice must be offered to the Heartwood. The question that will haunt me forever: who?
objectives: Choose who will be sacrificed to the Heartwood

SACRIFICE_SELF.journal_entry: I offered myself. The Heartwood accepted. As the roots wrap around me, I feel the forest's gratitude — alien, vast, ancient. Ashvale will live. I will become part of something older than memory.

SACRIFICE_LORD.journal_entry: Lord Ashworth stepped forward. 'My family made this pact,' he said. 'My family broke it. Let it end with me.' The roots took him gently — more gently than I expected. He smiled at the last. The haunted man found peace.

SACRIFICE_MAREN.journal_entry: Brother Maren knelt before the Heartwood. 'I poisoned their water to weaken them, to make them need the Church. Let this be my penance.' The Ashen Light teaches that suffering purifies. Perhaps he finally believed it.

SACRIFICE_VILLAGER.journal_entry: I chose someone. A life for a village. They went willingly — or so I tell myself. The Heartwood does not care about the weight on my conscience. It only cares that the blood was given. This is the darkest thing I have ever done.

SEVERANCE_PATH.journal_entry: I will burn the blood-root with druidfire. Old Maren says the ritual will sever the connection permanently — but the cost is the forest itself. Every tree, every root, every living thing in the Thornwood will die.
objectives: Learn the druidfire ritual from Old Maren
Fight through the forest guardians to reach the blood-root

SEVERANCE_BLOCKED_HEALTH.journal_entry: Old Maren collapsed before she could finish teaching me the ritual. Her connection to the forest is killing her — the very knowledge I need is draining the life from the only person who possesses it. I must find another way to learn the druidfire rite, or abandon the severance entirely.
objectives: Find another source for the druidfire ritual
Search the druid ruins for written instructions
Or choose a different path at the Heartwood

SEVERANCE_PREPARED.journal_entry: I know the ritual. The components are gathered. All that remains is to reach the blood-root at the heart of the Heartwood and set it ablaze. The forest will fight us every step of the way. It knows what we intend.

SEVERANCE_BLOCKED.journal_entry: Old Maren refuses to teach the druidfire ritual. She says destroying the forest would be a crime worse than the pact itself. I must find another way — or convince her.

SEVERANCE_COMPLETE.journal_entry: The blood-root is ash. The Heartwood's scream echoed for hours — felt in the bones more than heard. The forest is dying. In weeks, the Thornwood will be a graveyard of dead wood. Ashvale is free. The pact is broken forever. But the land remembers what we did.

RENEGOTIATION_PATH.journal_entry: I believe there is a third way. The Heartwood is ancient but not mindless — it wants something. If I can discover what the forest truly desires, perhaps I can offer it something better than blood.
objectives: Discover what the Heartwood truly wants
Gather the knowledge needed to propose new terms

RENEGOTIATION_INFORMED.journal_entry: I understand now. The forest doesn't hunger for souls — it hungers for space. For growth. The pact was crude — blood for tolerance. But what the Heartwood truly wants is room to expand. The dead farmland south of Ashvale... if the village surrendered those fields to the forest...
objectives: Propose new terms to the Heartwood: land for peace
Convince Ashvale's factions to surrender the dead fields

RENEGOTIATION_ANGERED.journal_entry: I proposed the wrong terms. The Heartwood's fury shook the ground — roots erupted around us, and we barely escaped. I need to understand what it actually wants before trying again.

RENEGOTIATION_BLOCKED.journal_entry: The factions refuse to surrender the farmland. Without their support, I cannot offer the Heartwood what it wants. I must find another path — or change their minds.

RENEGOTIATION_COMPLETE.journal_entry: The Heartwood accepted. New roots are already spreading into the dead fields, and the poisoned earth is beginning to heal. The village shrinks but survives. The forest grows but forgives. It is not a perfect peace — but it is the only peace that doesn't demand blood or death.
